hypothesis: If a single classifier-free guided score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state trajectories and used with score-based interpolation (isodensity or averaging) during the reverse SDE followed by Score-Aligned Ascent refinement with r_SAA = s^B - s^A, then it will generate transition-state configurations with committors near 0.5 and produce more accurate TS pathways across 2D potentials, alanine dipeptide, chignolin, and a chemical reaction than linear or geodesic interpolation baselines despite never being trained on TS data.
